title: Clibadium trianae
taxonomy: Clibadium trianae is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. More specifically, it belongs to the tribe Mutisieae and the genus Clibadium. The genus Clibadium is a group of flowering plants native to the Andes region in South America. Clibadium trianae is a relatively rare species found in the high-altitude regions of Colombia and Ecuador. As a member of the Asteraceae family, Clibadium trianae is related to other daisy-like plants and sunflowers.

The health benefits of Clibadium trianae include its potential to act as an anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antiparasitic agent, making it useful in the treatment of various health conditions such as fever, respiratory issues, and gastrointestinal problems.
